user_outcome: A model ID resolves through one explicit registry to a registered driver with fail-fast role checking — 'no driver for prefix X' and 'driver X cannot serve role Y' errors name what IS registered — and `openai/` now routes to the Responses API (sworn#31), with the sworn-proxy path resolving through the same visible registry.
rationale: Implements the 2026-07-02 registration + resolution decisions (intake): explicit DefaultRegistry(cfg) table wiring the compiled-in drivers (claude subprocess, codex subprocess, in-process oai, in-process responses), enumeration + availability probing, explicit prefix->driver mapping with no smart fallback. Grounding against live code: today there are THREE overlapping surfaces — model.NewClient's provider switch (internal/model/provider.go:87), model.FromEnv's keyless-then-proxy-then-direct routing (internal/model/config.go:40-53), and the hand-maintained capabilityRegistry (internal/model/registry.go:13) that can drift from actual Capabilities() methods. This slice makes the driver registry the single authority for LOOP dispatch resolution; NewClient/FromEnv remain only as the utility one-shot path's constructors (decided: one-shot is utility-only). The codex ErrDriverNotImplemented stub (provider.go:175-180) is removed here, closing the sworn#19 stub alongside S03's implementation. sworn#31: openai/ -> Responses driver, openai-completions/ -> legacy chat/completions, openai-responses/ kept as deprecated alias for one release. sworn#15 closes by subsumption. sworn#69 note: proxy routing stays deliberate S06b behaviour but becomes VISIBLE — enumeration shows when a prefix resolves through the proxy.
in_scope: internal/driver/registry.go: DefaultRegistry(cfg) explicit table + Resolve(modelID, role) fail-fast
Explicit prefix mapping incl. sworn#31 rename (openai/ -> Responses; openai-completions/ legacy; openai-responses/ deprecated alias)
Retiring the hand-maintained capabilityRegistry; re-pointing sworn capabilities at enumeration
Removing the provider.go codex ErrDriverNotImplemented stub
Proxy-routing visibility in enumeration; AGENTS.md + help-text updates
out_of_scope: FromEnv/NewClient as the one-shot utility path — kept for gates/bench

effort_complexity.rationale: Mechanics are decided and known-shape; the work is breadth — mapping, availability probing, the #31 rename spillover across help text/docs, and retiring the drifting capabilityRegistry.

risks.description: The #31 prefix rename breaks existing configs/specs/docs citing openai/ with chat/completions semantics.
risks.mitigation: openai-responses/ stays as a deprecated alias for one release; a repo-wide grep for openai/ citations is part of the slice; resolution errors name the new prefixes; AGENTS.md and help text updated in the same diff.

risks.description: Retiring the capabilityRegistry breaks `sworn capabilities` consumers.
risks.mitigation: `sworn capabilities` is re-pointed at registry enumeration in the same slice — one authority, no dark table left behind.

acceptance_criteria.text: internal/driver/registry.go SHALL provide DefaultRegistry(cfg) explicitly registering the compiled-in drivers (claude-cli subprocess, codex subprocess, in-process oai, in-process responses) with their prefix mappings, and Resolve(modelID string, role Role) (Driver, error) as the single resolution authority for loop dispatch; the hand-maintained capabilityRegistry table in internal/model/registry.go SHALL be deleted, with `sworn capabilities` re-pointed at registry enumeration.

When Resolve is called with a prefix that has no registered driver, it SHALL return an error naming the unknown prefix AND enumerating the registered prefixes, before any dispatch; TestResolveUnknownPrefix asserts the error text contains the full registered list.

When the resolved driver does not declare the requested role in its RoleSet, Resolve SHALL fail fast with an error naming the driver, the missing role, and which registered drivers DO declare it; there SHALL be no fallback to a different driver (explicit prefix, no magic — decided 2026-07-02).

The prefix mapping SHALL route openai/ to the Responses driver, openai-completions/ to the legacy chat/completions driver, and openai-responses/ as a deprecated alias of openai/ that resolves with a deprecation warning (sworn#31); the codex ErrDriverNotImplemented stub in internal/model/provider.go SHALL be removed; AGENTS.md and command help text SHALL reflect the new prefixes.

When the registry enumerates (Drivers()), each entry SHALL report the driver name, its prefixes, its RoleSet, and its availability in this environment (CLI binary found on PATH / API key or proxy login present) WITHOUT making any model dispatch; when proxy login is active and SWORN_DIRECT is unset, enumeration SHALL show which prefixes resolve through the proxy (the routing is visible, sworn#69).
